# Transcrição: Work_shouldn't_be_just_about_making_money

- **Fonte:** Work_shouldn't_be_just_about_making_money
- **Duração:** 00:58
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 00:58]

one piece of advice I remember hearing from one of my early mentors, he said that if the only benefit you're getting from your job is a paycheck, that's not enough. Financial reward is one of the rewards. You know, you should be trying to upskill, experience growth, fulfillment. There's so much more you could get from your job, not just the financial reward. For me, when I was young, the only thing that linked to a job was the dollar amount. And what will make me choose this job over that job is the dollar amount. I didn't realize there were so many other things I could get from my career, from my job. Yeah, I remember hearing a speaker at maybe uni or soon after it, he's like, by the time you're 50, how many paychecks will you truly remember? Because it took you 50 hours every week to get that paycheck. Like how many of those are memorable? Do you have any photos off it? Will you have it posted up on your wall? But what you will remember is where you're working on something meaningful. Where you're working with a team that you really enjoy it. Where you're doing things that you love. Or did it help you do things for your family?
